spec_match.rationale: Reads navigator.cpuPerformance as a numeric attribute and shows fallback state when unavailable.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP clicked Read tier again and Run benchmark; the second-read, stability, and benchmark fields updated.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still matches the index promise of API readout, companion signals, and benchmark comparison.

edge_cases.rationale: The page now performs a second attribute read and reports whether the value is stable or falling back.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature link in the see-also section.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout and keeps button color pairs explicit.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Fresh Chrome DevTools MCP v152 sweep loaded the copied concept, exercised every detected control, found named controls, and reported no post-triage runtime/action failure.

conformance.rationale: A v152 suite now carries the same feature-ID/spec contract without modifying the immutable source-milestone suite.

summary: Device Tier Readout now displays first read, second read, stability, companion signals, and a benchmark. Chrome DevTools MCP verified the controls with no console errors.
